## Understand terms

##### Annualised returns

Average of the yearly returns of a mutual fund over a given period.

##### Absolute returns

The total return of a mutual fund over a given period.

## Understand terms

##### Expense ratio

A fee payable to a mutual fund house for managing your mutual fund investments. It is the total percentage of a company's fund assets used for administrative, management, advertising, and other expenses.

##### Tax

A percentage of your capital gains payable to the government upon exiting your mutual fund investments. Taxation is categorized as long-term capital gains (LTCG) and short-term capital gains (STCG) depending on your holding period and the type of fund.

##### Exit load

A fee payable to a mutual fund house for exiting a fund (fully or partially) before the completion of a specified period from the date of investment.

##### Stamp duty

A form of tax payable for the purchase or sale of an asset or security.

### Exit Load

18 Sep 2014

Exit load of 0.25% if redeemed within 7 days

01 Jan 2013

Exit load of 4% if redeemed within 90 days

### Exit load, stamp duty and tax

#### Exit load

Exit load of 0.25% if redeemed within 7 days

#### Stamp duty on investment: 0.005% (from July 1st, 2020)

from July 1st 2020

#### Tax implication

If you redeem within one year, returns are taxed at 20%. If you redeem after one year, returns exceeding Rs 1.25 lakh in a financial year are taxed at 12.5%.

### 

### About Tata BSE Sensex Index Direct

Tata BSE Sensex Index Direct is a Equity Mutual Fund Scheme launched by Tata Mutual Fund. This scheme was made available to investors on 30 Jun 1995\. Rakesh Prajapati is the Current Fund Manager of Tata BSE Sensex Index Direct fund. The fund currently has an Asset Under Management(AUM) of ₹2,25,507 Cr and the Latest NAV as of 17 Jun 2026 is ₹205.74\. The Tata BSE Sensex Index Direct is rated Very High risk. Minimum SIP Investment is set to ₹150\. Minimum Lumpsum Investment is ₹5,000\. Exit load of 0.25% if redeemed within 7 days;

#### Investment Objective

The scheme aims to provide medium to long term capital gains, by investing in equity shares of only those companies comprised in the BSE Sensex and in the same proportion as that of the index, regardless of their investment merit.

Fund benchmarkBSE Sensex Total Return Index
